children.heading: Field-based course
children.label: 1 Field-based course
children.text: The term “field-based course” means a course of instruction offered by an institution of higher education that includes a requirement that students teach a minimum of 3 lessons or sequences of lessons to elementary or secondary students.

children.heading: Institution of higher education
children.label: 2 Institution of higher education
children.text: The term “institution of higher education” has the meaning given that term by .section 1001 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: who has been awarded a master’s or doctoral degree by an institution of higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: whose graduate coursework included courses in mathematics, science, computer science, or engineering;

children.label: C
children.text: who has at least 3 years teaching experience in K–12 settings; and

children.label: D
children.text: whose teaching has been recognized for exceptional accomplishments in educating students, or is demonstrated to have resulted in improved student achievement.

children.heading: Mentor teacher
children.label: 4 Mentor teacher
children.text: The term “mentor teacher” means an elementary or secondary school classroom teacher who assists with the training of students participating in a field-based course.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 5 Director
children.text: The term “Director” means the Director of the National Science Foundation.
